about: I'm a professional Software Engineer looking to branch out into new employment opportunities. I pride myself on being very friendly and a good team player while also being able to work well when left to my own devices. No matter what I do or who I'm working with, my main goal and motivation is to deliver the best work I can and improve someone's day. I'm always eager to learn new skills and engage with new experiences.

experience.title: Registration
items: Volunteer helping to organize the annual Tora-Con, the largest anime/comic convention in upstate NY. I volunteered for each convention from 2016 to 2019.

From 2017 onwards, I was part of the registration team, where I helped handle advertising, vendor applications, attendance numbers, distributing badges, as well as handling all revenue from badge purchases. I was set to become the head of the registration team for 2020's Tora-Con, as the current head was graduating. However the pandemic cancelled Tora-Con that year.

experience.title: Cashier & Restocker
items: From 2015 to 2016, I had a summer job at The Beer Store. There I worked the till where I would take customer orders and fetch them from the cold storage in the back of the store. This was my first retail job and I learned the fundamentals of working in retail, such as customer service, handling unruly customers, and point of sale systems.

experience.title: Cashier & Food Prep
items: Worked as a cashier and prep cook during my time at RIT. I worked at the point of sale during high activity times like the lunch and dinner rushes, where I learned how to work efficiently and quickly during high stress times.

I also worked in the back doing food prep and other odd jobs like restocking, cleaning, and dishwashing. There I learned the importance of being able to take direction, and prioritize many tasks to work as efficiently as I can.

personal.name: Myplanet
personal.description: Web developer and UI lead on a project for a Telus Health, developing a high profile medical web application. DevOps lead launching a new eCommerce website for a foreign client.

personal.name: Psyop
personal.description: Worked in the Real-time Interactive group where I handled mocap data on a AAA video game project. Attended mocap shoots and processed all data for use in-game animations. Solo developer on various AR/XR proof-of-concepts.

personal.name: mPulse
personal.description: UI engineer for mPulse's portals division. I built and maintained responsive web and mobile UIs personalized for dozens of the largest healthcare clients in the US. Worked on project teams to develop new applications and features.

education.minors: Minored in Game Design & Development, Woman & Gender Studies
